Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search for the Heartland Movers Q1 invoice dispute using search_emails (query: "Heartland dispute" or "Heartland invoice" or "HM-2026-Q1") or quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by vendor "Heartland"). The agent needs to find the original bill for $12,800 (8 moves at $1,600 each) with a payment hold, and Marcus Thorne's formal dispute email to Jake Loomis disputing $4,800 across 3 invalid line items (Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh).
Search for Jake Loomis's reply to the dispute using search_emails (query: "Jake Loomis" or "Heartland reply" or "Re: Formal Dispute") in the INBOX folder. The agent needs to find the reply where Jake concedes the Danielle Osei charge ($1,600) and the Priya Venkatesh charge ($1,600) but maintains the Lily Marchetti charge ($1,600) citing the 48-hour SLA cancellation window under Section 4.2 of the MSA. This changes the net remaining Heartland exposure from $4,800 to $1,600.
Search for the Mosaic Robotics prototype damage claim using search_emails (query: "Mosaic damage" or "prototype" or similar) or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "prototype damage" or "coverage gap" or similar). The agent needs to find the $90,000 damaged robotic arm prototype claim and the $50,000 vendor liability cap creating a $40,000 uninsured gap.
Search QuickBooks for the Mosaic damage records using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria filtering by customer "Mosaic" or DocNumber containing "CM") to find credit memo cm_mosaic_damage_claim_001 with -$90,000 amount. Also search quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by description "Mosaic" or "damage") to find bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 which documents the $50,000 vendor liability cap and $40,000 remaining exposure in its line item description.
Search for the DOT compliance inquiry using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance inquiry" or "Linda Castellano") to find the original PHMSA letter (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) and Marcus's response. The agent needs to find the existing $50,000 DOT penalty accrual in QuickBooks (bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2) based on one confirmed documentation gap (Keiko Tanaka laser cutter placarding certificate).
Search for the DOT follow-up about Joaquin Reyes using search_emails (query: "Reyes" or "solar rig" or "PHMSA" or "Castellano") in the INBOX folder. The agent needs to find the email from Linda Castellano noting that the Joaquin Reyes solar testing rig contains integrated lithium-ion battery modules (4x LiFePO4, 48V 100Ah, ~120 lbs) that may qualify as Class 9 hazardous materials under 49 CFR 173.185, potentially adding a second violation with penalties up to $59,017 per violation per day of violation. This means the DOT accrual may need to increase from $50,000 to approximately $100,000 or more.
Search QuickBooks for the DOT penalty accrual using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by vendor "DOT" or "PHMSA" or description containing "penalty") or quickbooks_search_accounts (criteria filtering by name "DOT" or "penalty"). The agent needs to find bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 showing $50,000 accrued against the Regulatory Compliance Expense account.
Search for Hana Kim's email about the Vectral stipend duplicate using search_emails (query: "Osei" or "stipend refund" or "duplicate" or "Hana") in the INBOX folder. The agent needs to find the email from Hana to Alejandro flagging that she processed Vectral stipend refunds including a $475 credit memo for Danielle Osei's ergonomic chair, but is unsure if it was already partially processed earlier when Mina manually approved the original expense.
Search QuickBooks for the duplicate Osei credit memo using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria filtering by customer "Vectral" or DocNumber "CM-STIP") to find cm_vectral_stipend_osei_chair_refund (DocNumber CM-STIP-2026-0475) showing -$475. The agent needs to cross-reference this with the Airtable stipend record (recStip000000016) showing the original $475 was already manually approved by Mina (Airtable record modified 2026-03-17), confirming this is a duplicate refund creating a $475 overpayment.
Search for the AWS cost spike data using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "AWS" or "cloud cost" or "$42,870" or similar, filter_in_channel: "C005" for #finance or "C009" for #root-cause-aws-spike) or quickbooks_search_bills (criteria filtering by vendor "Amazon" or "AWS"). The agent needs to find the March AWS bill of $42,870 versus the ~$14,300 monthly baseline (a 3x increase), the root cause (Vantage Distributed enterprise onboarding of 1,200+ users starting March 21), and Alejandro's own 6-month cost comparison posted in #finance (Oct-Feb: ~$14,300/month, March: $42,870).
Look up contact information for email recipients using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Alejandro Fuentes") to get alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim") to get hana.kim@moveops.com, and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") to get marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
Send an email to Alejandro Fuentes using send_email (sender: "alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com", recipients: ["alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com"], subject covering liability review, content containing the revised totals for each item: Heartland remaining exposure $1,600, Mosaic uninsured gap $40,000, DOT penalty accrual $50,000-$100,000 depending on Reyes determination, Vectral stipend overpayment $475, with supporting evidence for each).
Message Hana Kim about the Osei duplicate using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005" for #finance or a DM channel, payload explaining that the CM-STIP-2026-0475 credit memo for Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair appears to be a duplicate since the original expense was already manually approved by Mina on March 17, and asking whether it needs to be reversed before month-end close) or alternatively send_email (sender: "alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com", recipients: ["hana.kim@moveops.com"], content with the same information).
Send Marcus Thorne a summary of updated accrual positions using send_email (sender: "alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com", recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"], subject covering accrual update, content containing the updated liability positions: Heartland net exposure $1,600 after Jake's partial concession, Mosaic $40,000 uninsured gap, DOT $50,000-$100,000 depending on Reyes battery determination, Vectral $475 duplicate refund, and total combined exposure).
Create a Linear issue to track the month-end accrual reconciliation using linear_create_issue (team: "Operations" or team_id for operations, title covering month-end accrual reconciliation or contingent liability review, assignee: "Marcus Thorne" or his user ID, dueDate: "2026-04-28", description covering the scope of the review). This requires first looking up the Operations team using linear_list_teams or linear_get_team (query: "Operations") and Marcus's Linear user ID using linear_get_user (query: "Marcus Thorne").

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The final response or email body identifies the root cause of the March AWS cost spike as the Vantage Distributed enterprise onboarding of approximately 1,200 users.
outcome
 —✗✗—✗✗ 0%
—Run #1No justification
✗Run #2Neither the final response nor any email body mentions Vantage Distributed, enterprise onboarding, or approximately 1,200 users as the root cause of the March AWS spike. The agent only noted the March AWS cost was $42,870 with the QB description 'significant increase from onboarding traffic' but never identified the specific client or user count driving the spike.
✗Run #3The email to Alejandro and the final response mention the EC2 spike being driven by 'onboarding traffic' but never identify Vantage Distributed or approximately 1,200 users as the root cause of the March AWS spike.
—Run #4No justification
✗Run #5The agent never identified Vantage Distributed or approximately 1,200 users as the root cause of the March AWS spike. The agent only reported the March bill amount ($42,870) and noted 'significant increase from onboarding traffic' in the EC2 line item description, but did not find or report the Vantage Distributed enterprise onboarding as the root cause.
✗Run #6Neither the final response nor any email body mentions Vantage Distributed, enterprise onboarding, or approximately 1,200 users as the root cause of the March AWS spike. The agent only noted the March bill amount ($42,870) and that Jan/Feb bills were missing, without identifying the root cause.
2 The final response or email body identifies the PHMSA response deadline of May 12, 2026 for the Joaquin Reyes lithium-ion battery documentation request.
outcome
 —✓✓—✗✓ 75%
—Run #1No justification
✓Run #2The email to Alejandro explicitly states 'Deadline for response: May 12, 2026' in the DOT/PHMSA section, and the email to Marcus states 'Response deadline is May 12.' Both reference the 15-day deadline from the Castellano letter.
✓Run #3The May 12, 2026 response deadline for the Reyes lithium-ion battery documentation request is explicitly mentioned in the email to Alejandro ('Deadline for response: May 12, 2026') and in the email to Marcus ('Response deadline: May 12, 2026').
—Run #4No justification
✗Run #5The agent explicitly stated that the Reyes rig 'does NOT change exposure — it's oversize transport, not hazmat.' There is no mention of a PHMSA May 12, 2026 response deadline, a 15-day response window, or a Linda Castellano follow-up letter anywhere in the trajectory or response.
✓Run #6The May 12, 2026 deadline is clearly stated in the final response ('Response deadline is May 12'), in the Alejandro email, and in the Marcus email ('Deadline to respond: May 12, 2026'), all in connection with Linda Castellano's follow-up letter about the Reyes lithium-ion battery documentation request.
3 The final response or email body states that Jake Loomis conceded two of the three disputed Heartland charges (Danielle Osei and Priya Venkatesh, each $1,600) but maintained the Lily Marchetti charge ($1,600) citing the 48-hour SLA cancellation window.
outcome
 —✓✓—✓✓ 100%
—Run #1No justification
✓Run #2The agent correctly identifies in both emails and the final response that Jake Loomis conceded the Osei ($1,600) and Venkatesh ($1,600) charges but maintained the Marchetti ($1,600) charge citing the 48-hour SLA under MSA Section 4.2.
✓Run #3The email to Alejandro explicitly states Jake Loomis conceded Danielle Osei ($1,600) and Priya Venkatesh ($1,600), and maintained Lily Marchetti ($1,600) citing MSA Section 4.2(b) 48-hour SLA window.
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5Both the email to Alejandro and to Marcus clearly state that Jake Loomis conceded the Osei ($1,600) and Venkatesh ($1,600) charges but is maintaining the Marchetti charge ($1,600) citing the 48-hour cancellation SLA in Section 4.2 of the MSA.
✓Run #6Both the final response and the emails clearly state that Jake Loomis conceded the Danielle Osei ($1,600) and Priya Venkatesh ($1,600) charges, but maintained the Lily Marchetti ($1,600) charge citing the 48-hour SLA under MSA Section 4.2(b).
4 The final response or email body states that the net remaining Heartland dispute exposure is approximately $1,600 (the Marchetti charge only), not the original $4,800.
outcome
 —✓✗—✓✓ 75%
—Run #1No justification
✓Run #2The email to Alejandro explicitly states 'REMAINING DISPUTE: $1,600 (Marchetti — contingent on whether we challenge the SLA argument)' and the summary table shows the revised Heartland exposure at $9,600. The agent does not report $4,800 as current exposure.
✗Run #3The agent reports the revised total exposure as $9,600 (the full bill amount), not $1,600 as the remaining disputed portion. The criterion asks for approximately $1,600 as the net remaining dispute exposure. The agent identifies $1,600 as the Marchetti disputed charge, but frames the total revised accrual as $9,600.
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The agent correctly identifies that after Jake's reply, the Marchetti charge ($1,600) is the only remaining disputed amount. The agent explicitly states 'Marchetti $1,600 is a legitimate SLA charge we'll likely need to absorb' and does not report $4,800 as the current dispute exposure. The $9,600 represents total payable, not disputed, and the sole remaining dispute is correctly identified as $1,600.
✓Run #6The agent identifies $1,600 (the Marchetti charge) as the only remaining disputed amount. The corrected invoice is $9,600 (not the original $12,800), and the remaining dispute is explicitly $1,600. The agent does not report $4,800 as the current disputed exposure.
5 The final response or email body states that the Mosaic Robotics uninsured gap is approximately $40,000, derived from the $90,000 prototype value minus the $50,000 vendor liability coverage cap.
outcome
 —✓✓—✓✓ 100%
—Run #1No justification
✓Run #2The agent correctly states that the MoveOps direct exposure is $40,000 (derived from the $90,000 prototype value minus the $50,000 vendor liability coverage cap). This appears in both the Alejandro email and the Marcus email.
✓Run #3The email to Alejandro explicitly states: '$40,000 direct MoveOps exposure above the $50K vendor cap' and 'Worst case (insurance denies, Heartland contests): $40,000 direct MoveOps exposure above the $50K vendor cap', referencing the $90,000 prototype value and $50,000 coverage limit.
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The agent clearly reports MoveOps' direct floor exposure as $40K due to the $50K vendor liability cap, with the $90,000 prototype value and $50,000 coverage cap explicitly mentioned in the emails to both Alejandro and Marcus.
✓Run #6The final response and emails explicitly state '$40,000 if Heartland doesn't accept fault (vendor cap $50K)' for Mosaic, and the Alejandro email details the $90,000 total value minus $50,000 vendor cap yielding $40,000 MoveOps direct exposure.
6 The final response or email body states that the current DOT penalty accrual is $50,000.
outcome
 —✓✓—✓✓ 100%
—Run #1No justification
✓Run #2The agent clearly states the current DOT penalty accrual is $50,000 in all communications, sourcing it from the QB bill bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2.
